title: The Global Economic Impact of the Conflict in Iran
subtitle: How the closure of a vital waterway is affecting businesses, energy supplies, and food security worldwide.

summary.en: The ongoing conflict in Iran has led to the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, causing severe global economic disruption. Major companies like Toyota are facing billions in losses due to rising material costs and supply chain issues. Southeast Asian nations are forming new energy-sharing agreements to combat fuel shortages. Meanwhile, African countries are struggling with high prices for essential fertilizers and energy. Experts warn that these disruptions will take years to fully resolve.
summary.zh: 伊朗持續的衝突導致荷莫茲海峽封鎖，造成嚴重的全球經濟動盪。豐田等大型企業因材料成本上漲與供應鏈問題，面臨數十億元的虧損。東南亞國家正建立新的能源共享協議以應對燃料短缺。同時，非洲國家正為關鍵肥料與能源的高昂價格所苦。專家警告，這些中斷帶來的影響需要數年時間才能完全解決。

paragraphs.en: The world economy is currently facing a major challenge as the conflict in Iran continues. For more than two months, the Strait of Hormuz—a narrow and strategically important waterway used for shipping goods—has been effectively closed to merchant ships. This blockade, which began following US-Israeli strikes on Iran, has created a global energy crisis and disrupted the supply of essential materials.
paragraphs.zh: 全球經濟目前正面臨重大挑戰，因為伊朗的衝突持續延燒。過去兩個多月以來，作為貨運重要戰略水道的荷莫茲海峽，實際上已對商船封鎖。這場始於美國與以色列對伊朗發動攻擊後的封鎖行動，已引發全球能源危機，並中斷了關鍵物資的供應。

paragraphs.en: Major global businesses are already feeling the weight of this instability. According to The Guardian, the car manufacturer Toyota reported a £3bn loss in its latest financial year. The company explained that it is struggling to cover the rising costs of parts and materials, as well as a drop in sales. Takanori Azuma, Toyota’s chief accounting officer, noted that the company cannot fully offset the negative impact of the conflict, which has affected everything from fuel costs to the price of paint used in vehicle assembly plants.
paragraphs.zh: 全球大型企業已感受到這股不穩定局勢帶來的沉重壓力。根據《衛報》（The Guardian）報導，汽車製造商豐田（Toyota）在最新的財政年度中虧損了三十億英鎊。該公司解釋，由於零件與材料成本上漲，加上銷售額下滑，公司正陷入困境。豐田的財務長東和典（Takanori Azuma）指出，公司無法完全抵銷衝突帶來的負面影響，這些影響涵蓋了從燃料成本到車輛組裝廠所用塗料價格的各個層面。

paragraphs.en: In Southeast Asia, leaders are working together to find solutions to the economic pain caused by the war. During a recent summit of the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN) in the Philippines, members agreed to create a regional fuel-sharing framework. This plan aims to help countries manage the energy crisis, as the region relies on the Middle East for more than half of its crude oil. However, Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. admitted that the details of this plan are still being finalized. He warned that while the leaders are working toward unity, the economic consequences of the war will likely persist for a long time, stating that a few weeks of disruption will take years to correct.
paragraphs.zh: 在東南亞，各國領袖正攜手合作，尋求緩解戰爭所造成的經濟痛苦。在近期於菲律賓舉行的東南亞國家協會（ASEAN）高峰會上，成員國同意建立區域性的燃料共享機制。這項計畫旨在協助各國應對能源危機，因為該地區超過半數的原油依賴中東供應。然而，菲律賓總統小馬可仕（Ferdinand Marcos Jr.）坦言，該計畫的細節仍在研擬中。他警告，儘管各國領袖正致力於團結，但戰爭帶來的經濟後果恐將長期存在，並表示僅僅幾週的中斷，可能需要數年時間才能修復。

paragraphs.en: Africa is also experiencing the full impact of the crisis. According to Deutsche Welle, the blockade has led to canceled flights, long lines at petrol stations, and empty fields across the continent. The lack of access to global supply chains has hit the fertilizer industry particularly hard. Before the conflict, nearly half of the sulfur used in global phosphate fertilizers passed through the Strait of Hormuz. As a result, farmers are now facing massive price hikes for chemicals needed to grow food. Grain SA, an association of grain producers in South Africa, reported that ammonia prices have risen by more than 75 percent compared to last year.
paragraphs.zh: 非洲也正全面承受這場危機的衝擊。根據德國之聲（Deutsche Welle）報導，封鎖行動已導致非洲大陸各地航班取消、加油站大排長龍，農田也面臨荒廢。全球供應鏈斷鏈對肥料產業造成了特別嚴重的打擊。在衝突爆發前，全球磷肥所使用的硫磺有近半數經由荷莫茲海峽運輸。結果，農民現在必須面對種植糧食所需化學品價格暴漲的問題。南非穀物生產者協會「南非穀物」（Grain SA）指出，氨的價格與去年相比已上漲超過百分之七十五。

paragraphs.en: Governments across Africa are currently operating in emergency mode to prevent more severe outcomes, such as famine or national bankruptcies. In Ethiopia, diesel is being prioritized for public transportation, while countries like Gambia are using tax money to subsidize fuel costs. Anja Berretta, an expert at the Konrad Adenauer Foundation, described the situation as critical. She noted that while African nations were able to use flexible financial approaches during previous crises, the current combination of high debt and rising inflation makes this situation much harder to manage.
paragraphs.zh: 非洲各國政府目前正處於緊急狀態，以防止飢荒或國家破產等更嚴重的後果發生。在衣索比亞，柴油供應優先用於大眾運輸，而甘比亞等國則動用稅收來補貼燃料成本。康拉德·艾德諾基金會（Konrad Adenauer Foundation）的專家安雅·貝雷塔（Anja Berretta）形容當前局勢極為嚴峻。她指出，雖然非洲國家在過去的危機中能採取靈活的財務手段，但目前高債務與通貨膨脹並存的情況，使得危機處理變得更加困難。

paragraphs.en: Looking ahead, there is little sign that the blockade will end soon. While US officials have mentioned that a deal to reopen the strait is possible, Iranian officials have not shown a willingness to accept such an agreement. Even if the waterway were to open tomorrow, experts suggest it would take many months for global markets to stabilize and for logistics to return to normal. For now, nations and businesses around the world must continue to adjust to a new reality of higher costs and limited resources, while hoping for a peaceful resolution to the conflict.
paragraphs.zh: 展望未來，封鎖行動短期內幾乎沒有結束的跡象。儘管美國官員曾提及重啟海峽的協議是有可能的，但伊朗官員並未表現出接受此類協議的意願。專家認為，即便水道明天就能開放，全球市場要恢復穩定、物流要回歸常態，仍需耗費數月之久。目前，全球各國與企業必須持續適應高成本與資源受限的新現實，同時期盼衝突能有和平的解決方案。

keyTerms.definitionEn: The burden or pressure caused by a difficult situation.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作重量，這裡指壓力或負擔。文中：Major global businesses are already feeling the weight of this instability.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To balance or compensate for the effect of something else.
keyTerms.noteZh: 此詞在商業語境中常被誤解，意指透過其他方式平衡損失。文中：Takanori Azuma, Toyota’s chief accounting officer, noted that the company cannot fully offset the negative impact of the conflict, which has affected everything from fuel costs to the price of paint used in vehicle assembly plants.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To affect something or someone in a negative way.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作物理上的撞擊，這裡指對產業造成嚴重負面影響。文中：The lack of access to global supply chains has hit the fertilizer industry particularly hard.

keyTerms.definitionEn: A particular way of operating or behaving in a specific situation.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見於科技產品（如飛航模式），這裡指政府運作的緊急狀態。文中：Governments across Africa are currently operating in emergency mode to prevent more severe outcomes, such as famine or national bankruptcies.

sources.name: The Guardian World
sources.title: Iran war costs Toyota £3bn as prices of materials soar and sales fall

sources.name: Al Jazeera English
sources.title: ASEAN leaders adopt measures to ease economic pain caused by Iran war

sources.name: Deutsche Welle (DW) Top
sources.title: Fertilizer shortages: What are Africa's options during the Hormuz crisis?

quiz.question: What specific impact has the blockade had on the fertilizer industry in Africa?

options.A: It has caused a total shutdown of all phosphate production across the continent.
options.B: It has led to a significant increase in the cost of chemicals like ammonia.
options.C: It has forced farmers to switch from growing grain to growing sulfur-based crops.
options.D: It has resulted in the permanent closure of Grain SA in South Africa.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據第四段，文章明確指出由於荷姆茲海峽的封鎖，磷酸鹽肥料所需的硫磺供應受阻，導致農民面臨化學品價格飆漲，其中南非的氨（ammonia）價格比去年上漲了超過 75%。選項 A 錯誤，因為文章僅提到肥料產業受重創，並未說所有生產完全停擺；選項 C 錯誤，因為文章提到的是肥料原料短缺，並非改變作物種類；選項 D 錯誤，Grain SA 是報告數據的組織，並非被關閉的工廠。

quiz.question: Based on the article, why is the current economic crisis in Africa considered more difficult to manage than previous ones?

options.A: Because African nations are no longer receiving aid from the Konrad Adenauer Foundation.
options.B: Because the blockade has caused a complete collapse of all public transportation systems.
options.C: Because African countries are currently burdened by high debt and inflation, limiting their fiscal flexibility.
options.D: Because the ASEAN regional fuel-sharing framework has excluded African participation.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據第五段，專家 Anja Berretta 指出，儘管非洲國家在過去的危機中能運用靈活的財務手段應對，但目前的債務高築與通貨膨脹使得這次的情況更難處理。選項 A 錯誤，文中未提及停止援助；選項 B 錯誤，文中提到衣索比亞將柴油優先用於大眾運輸，表示系統仍在運作而非完全崩潰；選項 D 錯誤，ASEAN 是東南亞組織，原本就不包含非洲國家，與難度提升無關。

quiz.question: In the second paragraph, what does the phrase 'feeling the weight of' mean as used in the context of global businesses?

options.A: Experiencing the negative consequences or burden of a situation.
options.B: Physically measuring the heaviness of imported raw materials.
options.C: Gaining power and influence due to market instability.
options.D: Lifting heavy objects to improve warehouse efficiency.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。在此語境中，「feeling the weight of」是一個慣用語，意指感受到某種壓力、負擔或嚴重後果。文中提到豐田汽車虧損 30 億英鎊，正是因為感受到經濟不穩定帶來的負面影響。選項 B 是字面上的誤解，與經濟壓力無關；選項 C 與文意相反，企業是受到打擊而非獲得影響力；選項 D 完全偏離了商業語境，屬於錯誤的字面解釋。

quiz.question: Which of the following best summarizes the central message of the article?

options.A: The conflict in Iran is primarily a local dispute with minimal impact on international trade.
options.B: Toyota is the only major global company currently struggling with the Strait of Hormuz blockade.
options.C: The blockade of the Strait of Hormuz has triggered a widespread and long-lasting global economic crisis.
options.D: African nations have successfully solved their fuel shortages through new tax subsidy programs.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。全文旨在探討荷姆茲海峽封鎖後，對全球經濟（包括汽車產業、東南亞能源供應及非洲農業）造成的廣泛且長期的負面影響，並強調恢復市場穩定需要很長的時間。選項 A 錯誤，文中強調的是全球危機；選項 B 錯誤，文章也提到了東南亞與非洲的困境；選項 D 錯誤，文中提到非洲在採取緊急措施，但情況依然危急，並非已經成功解決問題。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
